8ro til ,90
OFFICE OF .IXE
CITY ATTORNEY
January 4, 1990
Kathryn E. Donovan, General Counsel
Fai r Poiitical Practices Conrnission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.0. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804
Re: Requesi for Ativ ice
Dear Ms. Donovan:
This is a request for advice concerning
a city councilmembelin relation to the
program.
Facts
potentia'l confl icts of interest of
Ci ty' s unreinforced masonry building
State iaw relating to seismic safety required cities to identify unreinforced
masonry buildings prior to January 1, 1990, and to report this information
to the state. South San Francisco has done thjs. State law further requires
the city to adopt a mitigation program; however, the type of program is left
to the discretion of the city and may be as simple as notifying each building
owner that the building is potentially hazardous or as compiex as requiring
an engineering analysis and retrofitting the building to conform to current
seismic requirements. South San Francisco is beginning to formulate a
mitigation program.
The City has identjfied some 25 privately owned buildings in roughly a three-
block area downtown which could be subject to a mitigation program under the
law. It also has been suggested that such a mitigation program should not
be limited to only the buildings of concern under the state law.
Unce the staff finishes formulating mjtigation alternatives it intends to
present the options to the Council at a study session. Depending upon what
occurs, it is likely that an ordinance will then be drafted and presented
to the Council for formal action.
Recently eiected councilmember John Penna is a real estate broker. Hjs
business, Penna Realty, deals wiih many properties throughout the City and
serves as the property manager for one of the bui'ldings on the 1ist. Hjs
315 l,laple Avenue 94080
P.0. Box 7ll 94083
City tial I Annex
(415) 877-8s15


Kathryn E. Donovan
January 4, 1990
Page 2
business also has been assisting in seeking a tenant for another one
of the buildings on the list, although there is no on-going business
relationship. He, along wjth the other councilmembers, is interested
in devising a program which will improve public safety, particularly
in light of the recent earthquake.
Ques t i ons
1. May Councilmember Penna participate in devising and adopting
a program to deal with the identified unreinforced masonry
bui ldings?
2. May Councilmember Penna participate in determining if a
seismic safety program should be applied City-wide and
participate in devising and adopting such a prognam'i
Thank you in advance for the time and attention devoted to this request.
If you need any additional information or clarification, do not hesitate
to contact me.
Very truly yours,
oL-"*4, 2..-,---*
VALERIE J. ARMENTO
City Attorney

OFFICE OF THE
CITY ATTORNEY
April 23, 1990
Jeevan Ahuja
Fair Pol itical Practices Cormission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.0. Box 807
Sacramento, CA. 95804
Re: Letter No. 90-032
Dear trlr. Ahuja:
0n January 4, 1990 I sent a request for advice, which the FPPC received on
July 8. By way of a January 10 letter from Kathryn Donovan I was infornnd
that you were assigned to this matter and that typically a response is
received within 21 working days.
0n February I, you and I had a telephone conversation during which you sought
clarification of some points. I have not heard from you since, despite a
phone call approximately one ronth ago and another 'last week,
t'ly City Council will be holding a public hearing on the matter of adopting an
unreinforced masonry structure ordinance on t'lay 23, 1990. I'lhether or not one
of ny Councilmembers may participate in that is the primary reason the opinion
was requested. It would be extremly helpful if we could have a response
before that date.
Very truly you rs,
zIz;A.e*.4
VALERIE J. ARTENTO
City Attorney
